2606.27091.title: Inherited Circuits, Learned Semantics: How Fine-Tuning Creates Evasion Vulnerabilities Invisible to Standard Evaluation
2606.27091.paper_summary: LLMs fine-tuned for security classification are usually evaluated on held-out examples from the same distribution as their training data. We show that this can miss vulnerabilities introduced by fine-tuning itself: models can learn token-level indicator semantics that preserve canonical accuracy while failing under behavior-preserving transformations such as PowerShell alias substitution, command reconstruction, string construction, execution indirection, and case mutation. We study Foundation-Sec-8B-Instruct and its base model, Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct, on matched PowerShell classification cohorts. Causal interventions localize the classification circuit to a late-attention route inherited from Llama rather than created by fine-tuning. Fine-tuning concentrates and semantically specializes this inherited structure, improving baseline behavior while creating transformation-sensitive attack surfaces. A three-tier evasion benchmark finds Foundation-Sec misses on iwr substitution, Invoke-Expression reconstruction, and case-mutated Invoke-Expression/IEX variants that Llama does not share. We also derive a pre-deployment monitoring method: a linear probe at the classification boundary and an indicator-token sign test identify command families where canonical indicators change role after fine-tuning. These signals prioritize red-team variant generation using only canonical inputs, showing that security fine-tuning can improve task accuracy while expanding the evasion surface. These results caution against treating small task-specific fine-tunes as straightforwardly safer security classifiers: specialization can convert inherited model structure into brittle indicator rules that preserve held-out accuracy while expanding the evasion surface. Robust AI-enabled security will require specifying the full transformation space of the task and monitoring semantic drift through fine-tuning.
2606.27091.paper_summary_zh: LLMs 專為安全分類進行微調，通常在與其訓練數據相同分佈的保留範例上進行評估。<lb/>我們展示這可能會錯過微調本身引入的漏洞：模型可以學習保留典範準確性的標記級指標語義，但在行為保留轉換下失敗，例如 PowerShell 別名替換、命令重建、字符串構建、執行間接和大小寫變異。<lb/>我們研究 Foundation-Sec-8B-Instruct 及其基礎模型 Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct，在匹配的 PowerShell 分類群體上進行實驗。<lb/>因果干預將分類電路定位到從 Llama 繼承的晚期注意路徑，而不是由微調創建的。<lb/>微調集中並語義專門化這一繼承結構，改善基線行為，同時創造對轉換敏感的攻擊面。<lb/>一個三層逃避基準發現 Foundation-Sec 在 iwr 替換、Invoke-Expression 重建和大小寫變異的 Invoke-Expression/IEX 變體上存在漏失，而這些是 Llama 所不具備的。<lb/>我們還推導出一種預部署監控方法：在分類邊界進行線性探測，並使用指標標記符號測試來識別在微調後角色改變的命令家族。<lb/>這些信號優先考慮使用僅包含典範輸入的紅隊變體生成，顯示安全微調可以提高任務準確性，同時擴大逃避面。<lb/>這些結果提醒我們，不應將小型任務特定的微調視為簡單安全的安全分類器：專門化可能會將繼承的模型結構轉化為脆弱的指標規則，這些規則在保持保留準確性的同時擴大逃避面。<lb/>強健的 AI 驅動安全將需要指定任務的完整轉換空間，並通過微調監控語義漂移。
2606.27091.author: Ryan Fetterman et.al.
